source_intake_record.raw_question_text: With the aid of tunable lasers, Rydberg atoms of sodium have been produced with \( n \approx 100 \). The resulting atomic diameter would correspond in hydrogen to \( n \approx 600 \). What would be the speed of the electron in that orbit?

metadata has requires_image=true and text_dominant=false, so some image reliance is indicated by upstream labeling

multi_step: solving requires connecting the stated hydrogen-equivalent orbit n≈600 to the orbital-speed relation for Bohr-like atoms
this is more than direct recall because the problem combines a given correspondence with a physics formula/application step
multiple-choice format limits open-ended complexity, so the reasoning depth appears moderate rather than high
verifiability: answer is a specific numeric value with units and also appears among 4 answer choices
the expected result can be checked against a standard formula for electron speed in a hydrogen orbit
